text: The only way to do great work is to love what you do.
author: Steve Jobs

text: It is not the strongest of the species that survives, nor the most intelligent, but the one most responsive to change.
author: Charles Darwin

text: The unexamined life is not worth living.
author: Socrates

text: Imagination is more important than knowledge. Knowledge is limited. Imagination encircles the world.
author: Albert Einstein

text: In three words I can sum up everything I've learned about life: it goes on.
author: Robert Frost

text: Success is not final, failure is not fatal: it is the courage to continue that counts.
author: Winston Churchill

text: Outside of a dog, a book is man's best friend. Inside of a dog, it's too dark to read.
author: Groucho Marx

text: The best and most beautiful things in the world cannot be seen or even touched — they must be felt with the heart.
author: Helen Keller

text: It does not matter how slowly you go as long as you do not stop.
author: Confucius

text: I think, therefore I am.
author: Rene Descartes

text: The mind is everything. What you think you become.
author: Buddha

text: Creativity is intelligence having fun.
author: Albert Einstein

text: I have not failed. I've just found 10,000 ways that won't work.
author: Thomas Edison

text: The greatest glory in living lies not in never falling, but in rising every time we fall.
author: Nelson Mandela

text: Life is what happens when you're busy making other plans.
author: John Lennon

text: Two things are infinite: the universe and human stupidity; and I'm not sure about the universe.
author: Albert Einstein

text: The only true wisdom is in knowing you know nothing.
author: Socrates

text: Being deeply loved by someone gives you strength, while loving someone deeply gives you courage.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Success usually comes to those who are too busy to be looking for it.
author: Henry David Thoreau

text: The important thing is not to stop questioning. Curiosity has its own reason for existing.
author: Albert Einstein

text: You miss 100% of the shots you don't take.
author: Wayne Gretzky

text: To live is the rarest thing in the world. Most people exist, that is all.
author: Oscar Wilde

text: I can resist everything except temptation.
author: Oscar Wilde

text: Knowing yourself is the beginning of all wisdom.
author: Aristotle

text: Every artist was first an amateur.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Somewhere, something incredible is waiting to be known.
author: Carl Sagan

text: He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: It always seems impossible until it's done.
author: Nelson Mandela

text: Where there is love there is life.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

text: The secret of getting ahead is getting started.
author: Mark Twain

text: Believe you can and you're halfway there.
author: Theodore Roosevelt

text: The purpose of life is not to be happy. It is to be useful, to be honorable, to be compassionate.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: I'm not afraid of death; I just don't want to be there when it happens.
author: Woody Allen

text: The only thing I know is that I know nothing.
author: Socrates

text: Turn your wounds into wisdom.
author: Oprah Winfrey

text: The chief enemy of creativity is good sense.
author: Pablo Picasso

text: Nothing in life is to be feared, it is only to be understood.
author: Marie Curie

text: Our greatest weakness lies in giving up. The most certain way to succeed is always to try just one more time.
author: Thomas Edison

text: You know you're in love when you can't fall asleep because reality is finally better than your dreams.
author: Dr. Seuss

text: Don't be afraid to give up the good to go for the great.
author: John D. Rockefeller

text: What lies behind us and what lies before us are tiny matters compared to what lies within us.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Get your facts first, then you can distort them as you please.
author: Mark Twain

text: The journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Life is really simple, but we insist on making it complicated.
author: Confucius

text: Have no fear of perfection — you'll never reach it.
author: Salvador Dali

text: Science is a way of thinking much more than it is a body of knowledge.
author: Carl Sagan

text: Man is the only creature who refuses to be what he is.
author: Albert Camus

text: Perseverance is not a long race; it is many short races one after the other.
author: Walter Elliot

text: The best thing to hold onto in life is each other.
author: Audrey Hepburn

text: I find that the harder I work, the more luck I seem to have.
author: Thomas Jefferson

text: Act as if what you do makes a difference. It does.
author: William James

text: If you tell the truth, you don't have to remember anything.
author: Mark Twain

text: In the end, it's not the years in your life that count. It's the life in your years.
author: Abraham Lincoln

text: Behind every great man is a woman rolling her eyes.
author: Jim Carrey

text: The function of education is to teach one to think intensively and to think critically.
author: Martin Luther King Jr.

text: Creativity takes courage.
author: Henri Matisse

text: The good thing about science is that it's true whether or not you believe in it.
author: Neil deGrasse Tyson

text: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
author: Will Durant

text: Fall seven times, stand up eight.
author: Japanese Proverb

text: Love is composed of a single soul inhabiting two bodies.
author: Aristotle

text: The way to get started is to quit talking and begin doing.
author: Walt Disney

text: Whether you think you can or you think you can't, you're right.
author: Henry Ford

text: Do not dwell in the past, do not dream of the future, concentrate the mind on the present moment.
author: Buddha

text: Not all those who wander are lost.
author: J.R.R. Tolkien

text: A day without laughter is a day wasted.
author: Charlie Chaplin

text: Happiness is not something ready made. It comes from your own actions.
author: Dalai Lama

text: You can't use up creativity. The more you use, the more you have.
author: Maya Angelou

text: The saddest aspect of life right now is that science gathers knowledge faster than society gathers wisdom.
author: Isaac Asimov

text: A river cuts through rock not because of its power, but because of its persistence.
author: Jim Watkins

text: To love and be loved is to feel the sun from both sides.
author: David Viscott

text: Success is walking from failure to failure with no loss of enthusiasm.
author: Winston Churchill

text: It is during our darkest moments that we must focus to see the light.
author: Aristotle

text: The fool doth think he is wise, but the wise man knows himself to be a fool.
author: William Shakespeare

text: Life is either a daring adventure or nothing at all.
author: Helen Keller

text: I used to think I was indecisive, but now I'm not so sure.
author: Tommy Cooper

text: One cannot step twice in the same river.
author: Heraclitus

text: Art is not what you see, but what you make others see.
author: Edgar Degas

text: If I have seen further, it is by standing on the shoulders of giants.
author: Isaac Newton

text: It's not that I'm so smart, it's just that I stay with problems longer.
author: Albert Einstein

text: The giving of love is an education in itself.
author: Eleanor Roosevelt

text: Opportunities don't happen. You create them.
author: Chris Grosser

text: Start where you are. Use what you have. Do what you can.
author: Arthur Ashe

text: By three methods we may learn wisdom: first, by reflection, which is noblest; second, by imitation, which is easiest; and third, by experience, which is the bitterest.
author: Confucius

text: The purpose of our lives is to be happy.
author: Dalai Lama

text: Light travels faster than sound. This is why some people appear bright until you hear them speak.
author: Alan Dundes

text: The life of man is of no greater importance to the universe than that of an oyster.
author: David Hume

text: To create one's own world takes courage.
author: Georgia O'Keeffe

text: The most incomprehensible thing about the world is that it is comprehensible.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Courage is not having the strength to go on; it is going on when you don't have the strength.
author: Theodore Roosevelt

text: We accept the love we think we deserve.
author: Stephen Chbosky

text: It is better to fail in originality than to succeed in imitation.
author: Herman Melville

text: Everything you've ever wanted is on the other side of fear.
author: George Addair

text: The measure of intelligence is the ability to change.
author: Albert Einstein

text: You only live once, but if you do it right, once is enough.
author: Mae West

text: I told my wife she was drawing her eyebrows too high. She looked surprised.
author: Anonymous

text: Liberty consists in doing what one desires.
author: John Stuart Mill

text: The desire to create is one of the deepest yearnings of the human soul.
author: Dieter F. Uchtdorf

text: Equipped with his five senses, man explores the universe around him and calls the adventure Science.
author: Edwin Hubble

text: The oak fought the wind and was broken, the willow bent when it must and survived.
author: Robert Jordan

text: Love all, trust a few, do wrong to none.
author: William Shakespeare
